titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Camille Paglia Papers
titlestmt.author: Alison Fulmer

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 13, 2025 at 03:00

did.physdesc: (21 boxes) + 3 broadside, 222527571233 bytes (222.53 gigabytes) and 181 computer files.
did.abstract: The collection contains Paglia's professional papers including business and fan correspondence, recordings of her media interviews, written interviews, articles and columns written by Paglia, press material relating to Paglia and her work, and posters promoting her books and speaking events.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 24 contains restricted fragile computer media. Researchers must consult access copies instead of the originals. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Camille Paglia, 2022, 2023, 2024. " Sexual Personae publicity poster, 1990" donated in 2022. Hard drive containing media interviews donated in 2023. Additional papers donated in 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged in seven series: I. Correspondence, II. Media Work, III. Media Interviews, IV. Cover Stories, V. Press Clips, VI. Cartoons, VII. Posters.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Camille Paglia (1947- ) is an American academic, author, and social critic. She received a Master's in Philosophy in 1971 and a Ph.D. in 1974 from Yale University. She went on to teach at Bennington College in Vermont and at University of the Arts in Philadelphia. She has also worked as a columnist for Salon magazine and as a contributing editor for Interview magazine, and she currently serves on the Editorial Board for Arion: A Journal of Humanities and the Classics . Paglia has written and spoken extensively on social topics including feminism, sex, literature, film, fine arts, music, politics, and popular culture. Paglia identifies as a lesbian and as transgender, which she has discussed in media interviews. Her first book, Sexual Personae: Art and Decadence from Nefertiti to Emily Dickinson , published in 1990 by Yale University Press, explores sexual themes in art and literature. By 1991, the book had become a bestseller, bringing Paglia into the public eye.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Camille Paglia papers (MS 2134). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material from three accessions were combined during processing. See "Immediate Source of Acquisition" for more information. Original order has been maintained for correspondence, cover stories, press clips, and cartoons. Media work, media interviews, and posters have been organized chronologically to facilitate use. Newsprint in several series has been housed in mylar sleeves to mitigate acid transfer to other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains Paglia's professional papers including business and fan correspondence, recordings of her media interviews, written interviews, articles and columns written by Paglia, press material relating to Paglia and her work, and posters promoting her books and speaking events. The collection does not include any manuscripts of her books.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Camille Paglia papers is the physical property of the Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Paglia's business and fan correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged by year as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes articles and columns written by Paglia, written interviews with Paglia, correspondence about her media work, invitations to interview, permissions to use excerpts from her writings and interviews, media contracts, and trip itineraries. "Press about Paglia" folders include material referencing Paglia and her work such as articles, book reviews, and advertisements for her books, media appearances, and lectures. This series includes clippings and print outs of magazines, newspapers, and journals on the subject of entertainment, media studies, sex, politics, social criticism, music, and Italian American heritage.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged by year. Material referencing Paglia and her work is grouped under "Press about Paglia".

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This series has been rearranged from its original order to group material by year and to separate out material referencing Paglia but not written by her.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders include material referencing Paglia and her work including articles, book reviews, and advertisements for her books, media appearances, and lectures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains digitized video recordings of Paglia's interviews and other media appearances from an external hard drive. There are also production notes from Paglia about the recordings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The external hard drive in this series is arranged in the following groupings representing the original order of folders on the external hard drive. Files in each folder are listed chronologically, which does not represent the arrangement of original computer files on the hard drive. Information about the original file directory can be found in the Other Finding Aid note.
External hard drive >Paglia DVD's >>Paglia DVDs 2 >Paglia DVDs 4 >CAM DVD's >Paglia DVD's transferred 2019 >Paglia DVDs 3 >Paglia DVDs 5 >Camille for hard drive

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a spreadsheet of Paglia's production notes about the media interviews on the external hard drive.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes cover stories in various magazines and newspapers written by or about Paglia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes clippings and print outs of writings by Paglia, writings by others about Paglia and her work, and advertisements for her books and events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The folders in this series are arranged as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes an envelope of photographs of Paglia as a child and young adult that were sent to C-SPAN, August 3, 2003.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains cartoons of Paglia featured in various magazines and newspapers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains posters promoting Paglia's books and speaking events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged chronologically. Posters under 9" x 15" are grouped together in the folder "Small posters".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains small posters and flyers (up to 9" x 15") advertising Paglia's speaking events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This publicity poster for Paglia's book Sexual Personae: Art and Decadence from Nefertiti to Emily Dickinson hung in the window at Brentano's bookstore on Fifth Avenue in New York City.

did.unittitle: "The Sexual Personae Tour 1991: A New Breed of Feminists" Madonna poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description from Paglia: "Crisis in the American Universities", September 19, 1991, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (my most important lecture; overflow crowd was in the thousands).

did.unittitle: Promotional poster for "Female Misbehavior" documentary collection
"On Educational Reform", University of the Arts
"The Future of the American University", Institute of Politics, John F. Kennedy School of Government, Harvard University
"The Reform Movement in Academe", The Philomathean Society Annual Oration, University of Pennsylvania
"America Needs a Female President", Yale Political Union, Yale University
"Drag-Queen Feminism? Madonna- the True Feminist?", University of Georgia
"American Education Reform", Institute of Politics, John F. Kennedy School of Government, Harvard University
"American Culture and Politics at the End of the Century", University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign
Pull-out poster from Lingua Franca magazine with cartoon parodying "The School of Athens"
"The Writer as Cultural Critic", Rowan University
Break, Blow, Burn book talk, Northern Virginia Community College
"Religion and the Arts in America", Colorado College
"The Legacy and Future of Feminism", Program on Constitutional Government, Harvard University
"Three Modern Women: Katharine Hepburn, Hope Montgomery Scott, and Tracy Lord", Cabrini College
"From Egyptian Death Mask to Social Selfie: How Innovation Drives the Art of Portraiture", School of Art, University of Cincinnati
"Women, Ethnicity, and Race in Modern Media", Southern Connecticut State University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Full lecture title: "Women, Ethnicity, and Race in Modern Media: How minority women have been portrayed in the film and music industries from the 1920s to now".

did.unittitle: "Glennda and Friends" screening event, Queer Ass Film Club, Kasuri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description supplied by Paglia: "Glennda and Camille Do Downtown", screening of my 1993 video in Greenwich Village with Glenn Belverio (drag persona Glennda Orgasm), Queer Ass Film Club at Kasuri Design, Hudson, New York, November 17, 2023.
